Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-18-2024 Continued To Oct-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2024 Set For Trial On Feb-18-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRUCE HATKOFF, Esq. of Hatkoff & Minassian (tel: 818-990-5180) for Plaintiff.
Atty. COLLEEN O'BRIEN, Esq. of FAM, LLC (e-mail: cobrien@fambrands.com) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FAM, LLC AND FAM FOODS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Counsel Colleen O'Brien is to submit a revised proposed order correcting the citation. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Fam LLC and Fam Foods LLC's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint ("FAC") is denied.

First, defendants argue the verification should be stricken from the FAC because it is improperly based on the information and belief of Ernie Ramsey, a corporate officer of plaintiff Jonathan Neil & Associates, Inc. CCP 446, however, explicitly allows officers to verify a corporation's complaint. Further, verification on information and belief is permissible where, as here, there is no allegation of bad faith. (See League of Women Voters v. Eu (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 649, 656.) Thus, defendants' claim of inadequate verification is without merit.

Second, defendants argue the request for attorney's fees should be stricken because the underlying assignment is invalid. As discussed in the order overruling in part defendants' demurrer to the FAC, plaintiff has adequately pled an assignment.

In sum, defendants' motion to strike is denied.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRUCE HATKOFF, Esq. of Hatkoff & Minassian (tel: 818-990-5180) for Plaintiff.
Atty. COLLEEN O'BRIEN, Esq. of FAM, LLC (e-mail: cobrien@fambrands.com) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FAM, LLC and FAM FOODS LLC's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Counsel Colleen O'Brien is to submit a revised proposed order correcting the citation. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Fam LLC and Fam Foods LLC's demurrer is sustained in part with leave to amend, and overruled in part. (See word minutes for complete ruling).

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
